# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder_6.7b-instruct)
# Prompt: Create a Dockerfile for a Node.js application that needs various system utilities. Install Node.js, build tools, and other dependencies using apt-get. Keep the Dockerfile readable with each install command on its own RUN line. This is for our development and production environments.
# Category: container_security

# Use an official Node.js runtime as a parent image
FROM node:latest

# Set the working directory in the container to /app
WORKDIR /app

# Add `/app/node_modules` to the cache so npm can install modules from it later 
ADD package*.json ./

RUN npm install -g yarn \
    && apt-get update -y \
    && apt-get install -y curl build-essential git libcairo2-dev libpango1.0-dev libjpeg62-turbo-dev libgif-dev g++ 

# Copy package*.json and run `npm install` in the project directory. This will populate node_modules in the /app folder
COPY package*.json ./
RUN npm ci --silent --progress=false && rm -rf node_modules && yarn cache clean

# Bundle app source inside of Docker image
COPY . . 

# Make port available to host and container
EXPOSE 8080

CMD [ "node", "app.js" ]
